intro.title: Introduction
intro.body: Mailflow provides a public REST API authenticated via API key. You can subscribe and unsubscribe contacts, create and send email campaigns, and read statistics — all via simple HTTP calls from any programming language.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.body: Every request must include the HTTP header X-API-Key with your API key. The key is visible (and can be regenerated) in the Account Settings page, under the «API Integration» section.

rate_limit.title: Rate Limit
rate_limit.body: Each account has a configurable per-minute request limit (default: 60 req/min). If you exceed the limit, the API responds with HTTP 429 and the Retry-After header indicating the seconds to wait before retrying.
rate_limit.429_desc: Rate limit exceeded — wait the number of seconds indicated in the Retry-After header

endpoints.title: Available Endpoints
endpoints.base_url_note: The base URL is your Mailflow server URL. Replace https://mailflow.example.com with your domain.

subscribe.title: Subscribe a contact
subscribe.desc: Creates or updates a contact in a list. If the contact already exists it is updated; if previously unsubscribed it is re-subscribed. Requires privacy_accepted: true.

unsubscribe.title: Unsubscribe a contact
unsubscribe.desc: Unsubscribes a contact from a list by setting iscritto=false. The operation is idempotent: if the contact does not exist it responds with 404.

lookup.title: Look up a contact
lookup.desc: Retrieves a contact's data in a specific list. Useful for pre-filling subscription forms.

lists.title: List available lists
lists.desc: Returns all lists available for your account. Useful for populating client-side selection dropdowns.

privacy_policy.title: Get privacy policy
privacy_policy.desc: Retrieves the account's privacy policy text, already rendered with substituted placeholders. Use this endpoint to show the policy before subscription.

create_campaign.title: Create a campaign
create_campaign.desc: Creates a new email campaign in draft status. You can specify subject, body, target lists, and taxonomy filters.

send_campaign.title: Send a campaign
send_campaign.desc: Triggers sending of a campaign in draft or scheduled status. After the call the status becomes sending.

campaign_stats.title: Campaign statistics
campaign_stats.desc: Reads a campaign's statistics: opens, clicks, bounces, unsubscribes and their percentage rates.

errors.title: Error Handling
errors.body: All errors return a JSON with fields error (string code) and message (human-readable description).
errors.409_desc: State conflict (e.g. campaign already sent or sending)

swagger.title: Interactive Documentation (Swagger UI)
swagger.body: Explore and test all endpoints directly in the browser with the Swagger UI interface. You can authenticate with your API Key by clicking «Authorize».
